title: 391. Perfect Rectangle
question:
Given N axis-aligned rectangles where N > 0, determine if they all together form an exact cover of a rectangular region.

Each rectangle is represented as a bottom-left point and a top-right point. For example, a unit square is represented as [1,1,2,2]. (coordinate of bottom-left point is (1, 1) and top-right point is (2, 2)).

Example 1:

rectangles = [
  [1,1,3,3],
  [3,1,4,2],
  [3,2,4,4],
  [1,3,2,4],
  [2,3,3,4]
]

Return true. All 5 rectangles together form an exact cover of a rectangular region.

 

 

Example 2:

rectangles = [
  [1,1,2,3],
  [1,3,2,4],
  [3,1,4,2],
  [3,2,4,4]
]

Return false. Because there is a gap between the two rectangular regions.

 

 

Example 3:

rectangles = [
  [1,1,3,3],
  [3,1,4,2],
  [1,3,2,4],
  [3,2,4,4]
]

Return false. Because there is a gap in the top center.

 

 

Example 4:

rectangles = [
  [1,1,3,3],
  [3,1,4,2],
  [1,3,2,4],
  [2,2,4,4]
]

Return false. Because two of the rectangles overlap with each other.
code: class Solution(object):\u000A    def isRectangleCover(self, rectangles):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type rectangles: List[List[int]]\u000A        :rtype: bool\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        x1 \u003D y1 \u003D float(\u0027inf\u0027)\u000A        x2 \u003D y2 \u003D float(\u0027\u002Dinf\u0027)\u000A        area \u003D 0\u000A        pnt_cnt \u003D {}\u000A\u000A        for rect in rectangles:\u000A            a1, b1, a2, b2 \u003D rect\u000A            x1, y1 \u003D min(x1, a1), min(y1, b1)\u000A            x2, y2 \u003D max(x2, a2), max(y2, b2)\u000A            for x, y in [(a1, b1), (a2, b2), (a1, b2), (a2, b1)]: pnt_cnt[(x, y)] \u003D pnt_cnt.get((x, y), 0) + 1\u000A            area +\u003D (b2 \u002D b1) * (a2 \u002D a1)\u000A        \u000A        if area !\u003D (y2 \u002D y1) * (x2 \u002D x1): return False\u000A        \u000A        for pnt, cnt in pnt_cnt.items():\u000A            if pnt in [(x1, y1), (x2, y2), (x1, y2), (x2, y1)]:\u000A                if pnt_cnt.get(pnt, 0) !\u003D 1: return False\u000A            elif cnt % 2 !\u003D 0: return False\u000A        return True\u000A                    \u000A        \u000A        \u000A        \u000A
